markdownRemark.html:
The choerodon-ui large numbers solution uses the third-party bignumber.js library. For detailed usage, refer to the official website BigNumber

math Library

// Official online demo import method: import { math } from 'choerodon-ui/pro/lib/data-set';
// For actual projects, use the import method below
import { math } from 'choerodon-ui/dataset';

// 0.1 + 0.2 => 0.30000000000000004
math.plus(0.1, 0.2);  // 0.3

// 9007199254740992 + 1 => 9007199254740992
math.plus('9007199254740992', 1);  // BigNumber(9007199254740993)

// 9007199254740993 - 1 => 9007199254740991
math.minus('9007199254740993', 1);  // BigNumber(9007199254740992)

// strict
math.minus(new BigNumber('1234567'), 1);  // 1234566
math.minus(new BigNumber('1234567'), 1, { strict: true });  // BigNumber(1234566)

Application of Large Numbers in the Numeric Component

The numeric component of choerodon-ui supports displaying large numbers. The value can be passed as a string or a BigNumber object. The onChange hook parameter may be a number or a large number. For convenient subsequent calculations, please use the math library of choerodon-ui.

import { NumberField } from 'choerodon-ui/pro';
import BigNumber from 'bignumber.js';

function handleChange(value: number | BigNumber) {

}

return (
  <Form>
    <NumberField label="Supports passing strings" value="123456789.123456789" onChange={handleChange}  />
    <NumberField label="Supports passing BigNumber objects" value={new BigNumber('123456789.123456789')} onChange={handleChange}  />
  </Form>
);

FieldType.bigNumber

The fields in choerodon-ui's DataSet provide a bigNumber type. For ordinary number field types, the input in numeric components will automatically switch between number and BigNumber depending on whether the number overflows, whereas the data of the bigNumber field type is always a BigNumber object.

import { DataSet } from 'choerodon-ui/pro';

const ds = new DataSet({
    autoCreate: true,
    fields: [
      {
        name: 'age',
        type: 'number',
        step: 2,
        required: true,
        max: '12345678901234567890123456',
        min: '-12345678901234567890123456',
        defaultValue: '123456789012345678',
      },
      {
        name: 'big',
        type: 'bigNumber',
        step: 2,
        required: true,
        max: '12345678901234567890123456',
        min: '-12345678901234567890123456',
        defaultValue: '123456789012345678',
      },
    ],
  });

ds.current.get('age'); // BigNumber(123456789012345678)
ds.current.get('big'); // BigNumber(123456789012345678)

Remote Data Interaction

The mainstream method for remote data interaction now is using JSON format. When the system's built-in JSON library parses a JSON string with large numeric values, precision can be lost. Therefore, there are two solutions:

  1. Convert large numeric types to strings during interaction. DataSet does this by default. This method requires changes to the backend API, so it is not recommended.
  2. Use the json-bigint library for parsing and stringifying JSON. This library automatically converts large numbers in JSON to BigNumber objects and vice versa.

Note: json-bigint v1.0.0 has an issue where converted objects lose their prototype chain. Before this issue is fixed, please use version v0.4.0.

Individual Usage (This step is not needed if using global replacement)骤)：

import JSONBig from 'json-bigint';

// axios
axios.post(url, {
  data: {
    amount: new BigNumber('123456789.123456789')
  },
  transformRequest(data) {
    return JSONBig.stringify(data);
  },
  transformResponse(res) {
    return JSONBig.parse(res);
  }
});

// fetch
fetch(url, {
  method: 'POST',
  body: JSONBig.stringify({
    amount: new BigNumber('123456789.123456789')
  }),
}).then(res => res.text().then(text => JSONBig.parse(text)));

// ds
new DataSet({
  transport: {
    read: {
      transformRequest(data) {
        return JSONBig.stringify(data);
      },
      transformResponse(res) {
        return JSONBig.parse(res);
      }
    }
  }
})

Global Replace:

import JSONBig from 'json-bigint';
import JSON3 from 'json3';

// If a third-party library uses json3, the following steps need to be taken; otherwise, json3 will override the global JSON object.
JSON3.noConflict();
window.$JSON = window.JSON;
window.JSON = JSONBig;

// Compatible with fetch
if (typeof Response !== 'undefined') {
  Response.prototype.json = function json() {
    return this.text().then(text => JSON.parse(text))
  }
}

If the render return value is a number, it needs to be handled globally, otherwise an error will occur when converting to BigNumber:

import BigNumber from 'bignumber.js';

Object.assign(BigNumber.prototype, {
  [Symbol.iterator]() {
    return this.toString()[Symbol.iterator]();
  },
});

Internationalization

import { formatNumber } from 'choerodon-ui/dataset';
import BigNumber from 'bignumber.js';

Object.assign(BigNumber.prototype, {
  toLocaleString(lang, options) {
    return formatNumber(this, lang, options);
  },
});

Compatible lodash.isNumber

import BigNumber from 'bignumber.js';

Object.defineProperty(BigNumber.prototype, Symbol.toStringTag, {
  value: 'Number',
});

bigNumber.toFixed behaves the same as number.toFixed

  1. Supports string parameters
  2. When decimalPlaces is undefined, decimals are completely truncated
  3. toFormat needs to be properly handled, otherwise when calling toFormat(), the decimals will be discarded
import BigNumber from 'bignumber.js';

const { toFixed, toFormat } = BigNumber.prototype;
Object.assign(BigNumber.prototype, {
  toFixed(decimalPlaces, roundingMode) {
    const dp = Number(decimalPlaces);
    return toFixed.call(this, isNaN(dp) ? 0 : dp, roundingMode);
  },
  toFormat(decimalPlaces, roundingMode, format) {
    if (decimalPlaces === undefined) {
      decimalPlaces = this.dp();
    }
    return toFormat.call(this, decimalPlaces, roundingMode, format);
  },
});
